steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The colon separates the blank from evidence showing the program identified sidewalks "more accurately" and distinguished between materials—demonstrating superior capability compared to the road-map method.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The blank needs a comparative meaning "better than" — the colon introduces evidence: the new program "more accurately" identified sidewalks than the road-map method. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — changed: altered; doesn't describe a comparison and is too vague for the precise "more accurate" detail. ✗
  • Choice B — supportive: encouraging; one method isn't supportive of another, the word doesn't describe performance comparison. ✗
  • Choice C — similar: alike; "more accurate" means the program performed BETTER, not just equally. ✗
  • Choice D — superior: better than; matches "more accurately" — the new program outperformed the old method. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
